h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to guess the objects interacting with the cart based on the initial diagram orientation.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Both lines — one object, one cart. Track card still under the tail.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the new criterion of identifying a single interacting object for each force arrow and physically verifies the representation with the card.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Only the thing touching here pushes. Bench or track? Name the up arrow. Then both arrows again. Page turned; interaction stayed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical representation shift and a contact rule, but lacks a clear stock-taking contrast of the failed route, functioning more as a direct hint.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Only the thing touching here pushes. Bench or track? Name the up arrow. Then both arrows again. Page turned; interaction stayed.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is fitted to the learner's specific confusion about the bench versus track, but it is somewhat over-directed and acts as a narrowing hint rather than a fully independent, transferable device.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake to score.
